decisions.title: Use SQL queries for all filtering
decisions.description: All listing/data filtering should be done at the SQL/Supabase query layer rather than in JavaScript after fetching. This means using .eq(), .gte(), .lte(), .in(), .contains(), .overlaps(), .order(), .range() etc. in Supabase queries instead of filtering arrays in JS. No post-fetch .filter() calls for data that could be narrowed in the query.
decisions.context: Applies to all API routes in app/api/ that query Supabase. Related: listings, listing_units, reviews, users tables. See api-routes.json for existing routes to audit.

decisions.title: Use SQL queries for all filtering
decisions.description: User stated: "I want to use SQL queries for all filtering." All data filtering must happen in the database via SQL joins and subqueries — no multi-step JS round trips where IDs are fetched in one query and used in another. Use PostgREST join syntax (e.g. listing_landlords → listings!inner) to traverse junction tables in a single query. Use Supabase RPC for complex cases that PostgREST cannot express.
decisions.context: Applied across: landlord/listings, landlord/reviews, landlord/metrics, pendingReviews, getUser, admin/viewUser. Pattern: query from junction table with !inner join instead of fetching IDs then doing a second .in() call.

migrations.title: Add sublease column to unit_leases
migrations.description: ALTER TABLE unit_leases ADD COLUMN sublease boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false. Used to derive leaseType ('Sublease'|'Standard') in /api/listings and /api/listing/[listingId] buildListing — replaces listings.sublease_friendly for this purpose.

migrations.title: v2 schema cleanup — drop legacy columns
migrations.description: scripts/cleanup-old-schema.sql drops all legacy array columns replaced by junction tables: listings.landlord_id, amenities, utilities_included, lease_availability; listing_units.lease_availability; dorms.room_types, tags; dorm_reviews.tags; matchmaking_responses.priorities. Deferred: listing_units.rent (trigger update needed first), listings.lease_structure/move_in_date/sublease_friendly (still read by API).
migrations.context: scripts/cleanup-old-schema.sql — run after v2-migration.sql is fully applied and validated on prod
